card.intuition: A complex geospatial system that matches drivers and riders in real-time based on location, traffic, and supply/demand.
card.analogy: A giant game of 'Connect the Dots' on a map: every driver is a dot moving in real-time. When a rider appears, the system finds the 'nearest' moving dot that isn't busy. It's like a high-speed game of tag across a whole city.
when_to_use: Building Uber, Lyft, or Bolt clones
gotchas: Geospatial Sharding: the earth doesn't scale linearly. Some areas (Manhattan) have millions of events, while others (Deserts) have zero. Using 'S2 Geometry' or 'Geohashes' is essential for balancing load.
Write-heavy: drivers send location updates every few seconds. This requires a high-throughput ingestion layer (like Kafka).

exercise.prompt: Identify the index type commonly used to recursively divide geographic space into 4 quadrants for efficient proximity searching.
